item.title: Control simulations
item.description: Three popular control schemes are formulated and simulated. This page summarizes the main ideas, detailed treatments are in the PDFs (with accompanying Simulink implementations) linked under the section titles. Published: 2021-09-14 State Observer & Feedback Control PDF · Simulink A linear state observer is designed, specifically a Luenberger observer of the form: $$ \begin{dcases} \dot{\hat{\mathrm{x}}}=\mathrm{A} \hat{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{B} \mathbf{u}+\mathrm{L}(\mathbf{y}-\hat{\mathbf{y}}) \\ \hat{\mathbf{y}}=\mathrm{Cx} \end{dcases} $$ The observer gain matrix $\mathrm{L}$ is to be designed.

item.title: Convex optimization
item.description: These problems are handpicked from my projects in Convex Analysis & Optimization and Real Analysis. The solutions and proofs are my own and are linked as PDFs under the section titles. Published: 2021-09-13 Semicontinuity PDF Let $f : \R^n \to \R$ be a lower semicontinuous function, and $g : \R \to \R$ be a lower semicontinuous and nondecreasing function. Show that the function $h$ defined by $h(x) = g \circ f(x)$ is lower semicontinuous.

item.title: Defining a robot
item.description: This article discusses the nuances and difficulties in trying to define a robot. Published: 2021-08-20 History The word robot has literary origins (specifically, Czech) meaning forced labor, vaguely implying that a robot is a machine-worker. Curiously though, in the initial literary context, robots were described as artificial biological organisms, rather than the popular mechanical interpretation (e.g., automaton, device, machine). The definition of a robot changes across cultures and has changed throughout time.

item.title: General resources
item.description: I update this page regularly with resources that may be helpful to others. Learning and reference materials marked as “Online” are freely accessible. Published: 2022-03-01 · Revised: 2024-07-10 Learning Material Mathematics Foundations of Applied Mathematics · Book series by Humpherys & Jarvis Numerical Algorithms · Online book by Justin Solomon Mathematics for Machine Learning · Online book by Deisenroth, Faisal, and Ong

item.title: Matrix calculus
item.description: This article discusses notational practices in matrix calculus and recommends a mnemonic inspired by the outer product. Published: 2024-02-24 · Revised: 2024-07-02 Background and Motivation In the context of differentiation, Leibniz’s notation ${\big(\frac{\mathrm{d}f}{\mathrm{d}x}\big)}$ is typically used for functions ${f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}}$, i.e., scalar-valued function of a real variable, and is a shorthand representing ${\lim_{\Delta x \to 0} \frac{\Delta f}{\Delta x}}$. In higher-dimensional settings, a function can be multivariate (multidimensional input), vector-valued (multidimensional output), or both.

item.title: Posts
item.description: Articles Matrix calculus Analyzing the ‘matrix derivative’ QP and least squares Comparing QP with least squares Spatial transforms Interpreting rigid-body transforms Robot mechanics Reviewing robot kinematics and dynamics Defining a robot Discussing ‘what is a robot?’ Projects Convex optimization Problems with my proofs and solutions.

item.title: QP and least squares
item.description: This article relates the problem formulations of quadratic programming and least squares under linear constraints. Published: 2024-02-18 Background When formulating optimization problems in applied settings (e.g., robotics, vision), it may be unclear whether to frame them as a quadratic program (QP) or as a least squares (LS) problem. Typically, this has the structure of an inverse problem; briefly: given a desired output $\mathbf{y}_\mathsf{d}$ and a forward model ${\mathrm{G}: \mathbf{x} \mapsto \mathbf{y}}$, we seek the input $\mathbf{x}^*$ that is mapped closest to $\mathbf{y}_\mathsf{d}$.

item.title: Research
item.description: Publications [PDF] Cut Sequencing Algorithm for Safely Disassembling Large Structures, 2023 IEEE Conference on Decision and Control (CDC). James Akl; Sumanth Pericherla; Berk Calli. [PDF] Vision-Based Oxy-Fuel Torch Control for Robotic Metal Cutting, 2023 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS). James Akl; Yash Patil; Chinmay Todankar; Berk Calli. [PDF][Dataset] CNN-Based Task State Estimation for Safer Automation of Oxy-Fuel Metal Cutting, 2023 IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE).

item.title: Robot mechanics
item.description: This article covers a broad but formal understanding of concepts in robot kinematics and dynamics. Published: 2021-08-20 Motivation The basic functionality we’d like robots to have, is to move. However, describing a motion from point $\mathsf{A}$ to point $\mathsf{B}$ precisely is not straightforward. Consider these two points $\mathsf{A}$ and $\mathsf{B}$. There are infinite paths from $\mathsf{A}$ to $\mathsf{B}$. Same start and end positions, but different intermediate states. The gray paths are two alternatives to our desired one.

item.title: Spatial transforms
item.description: This article covers ways to interpret homogeneous transforms while recommending notational practices. Published: 2021-09-06 Homogeneous Matrices In robot kinematics, coordinate frames are commonly used to keep track of the position and orientation of entities in the space. A coordinate frame $\{O, (\hat{i}, \hat{j}, \hat{k})\}$ in 3D Euclidean space is defined commonly using an origin $O$ and orthonormal basis vectors $(\hat{i}, \hat{j}, \hat{k})$. With two frames $\mathsf{F}$ and $\mathsf{F}'$ defined, one can measure the relative position and orientation of one frame relative to another; or one can translate and rotate one frame relative to another.
